overview.title: Web Development Resources
overview.description: Master modern web development with React, Next.js, Node.js, and essential tools for building production-ready applications

resources.name: React 18+ Modern Development
resources.shortDesc: Component-based UI library with hooks, concurrent features, and massive ecosystem
resources.fullDescription: React is the most popular JavaScript library for building user interfaces, developed by Meta (Facebook). React 18 introduces concurrent rendering, automatic batching, transitions, and Suspense improvements. With its component-based architecture, unidirectional data flow, and virtual DOM, React powers millions of websites including Facebook, Instagram, Netflix, and Airbnb. The ecosystem includes state management (Zustand, Redux), routing (React Router), and UI libraries (shadcn/ui, Chakra).

features.Automatic Batching: Multiple state updates in one render
features.Transitions: Keep UI responsive during heavy updates

ecosystem.UI Libraries: shadcn/ui, Chakra UI, Material UI, Ant Design

bestFor: Projects requiring rich ecosystem and community

learningPath.Beginner: Learn JavaScript → React basics (components, props, state) → Hooks
learningPath.Intermediate: Context API → Custom hooks → React Router → Form handling
learningPath.Advanced: Performance optimization → Server Components → Concurrent features → Design patterns

codeSnippet.title: Modern React Component with Hooks

resources.name: Next.js 15 - React Framework
resources.shortDesc: Production-ready React framework with App Router, Server Components, and edge rendering
resources.fullDescription: Next.js is the most popular React framework for building full-stack web applications. Created by Vercel, it provides routing, server-side rendering (SSR), static site generation (SSG), API routes, image optimization, and more out of the box. Next.js 15 introduces the App Router with React Server Components, streaming, and improved performance. Powers websites like TikTok, Twitch, Hulu, and Nike.

specifications.CSS Support: CSS Modules, Tailwind CSS, Sass, CSS-in-JS

features.Edge Runtime: Deploy to edge for ultra-low latency

appRouterConcepts.Server Components: Default in App Router, run on server, zero JS bundle
appRouterConcepts.Layouts: Shared UI that doesn't re-render on navigation
appRouterConcepts.Parallel Routes: Show multiple pages in same layout
appRouterConcepts.Route Handlers: API routes with GET, POST, etc.

bestFor: Projects requiring API routes in same codebase

performanceFeatures.Automatic Code Splitting: Only load JS for current page
performanceFeatures.Image Optimization: Serve optimal image sizes and formats
performanceFeatures.Streaming: Start rendering before all data loads

codeSnippet.title: Next.js 15 App Router Example

resources.name: Node.js - JavaScript Runtime
resources.shortDesc: Server-side JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 engine for scalable network applications
resources.fullDescription: Node.js is a JavaScript runtime environment that executes JavaScript code outside of a web browser. Built on Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine, Node.js uses an event-driven, non-blocking I/O model that makes it lightweight and efficient. Perfect for building RESTful APIs, real-time applications, microservices, and CLI tools. Powers backend systems for Netflix, PayPal, LinkedIn, Uber, and NASA.
notGoodFor: Heavy computation (use Python, Go, or Rust)

codeSnippet.title: Express API with TypeScript

resources.name: TypeScript - JavaScript with Types
resources.shortDesc: Strongly-typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript for safer code
resources.fullDescription: TypeScript is a strongly-typed programming language that builds on JavaScript, developed by Microsoft. It adds optional static typing, interfaces, enums, generics, and advanced tooling to JavaScript. TypeScript catches errors at compile time before they reach production, improves code maintainability, and provides excellent IDE support with autocomplete and refactoring. Used by Airbnb, Slack, Google, Microsoft, and many Fortune 500 companies.

specifications.Adoption: Very High (80%+ of new projects use TypeScript)

benefits.Catch Bugs Early: Errors at compile time, not runtime
benefits.JavaScript Superset: All JS is valid TS (gradual adoption)

bestFor: Libraries and frameworks (type safety for users)
notFor: Very small scripts (overhead not worth it)
Quick prototypes (types slow you down initially)
Solo learning projects (vanilla JS might be simpler)

codeSnippet.title: TypeScript Advanced Features

resources.name: Tailwind CSS - Utility-First Framework
resources.shortDesc: Utility-first CSS framework for rapidly building custom user interfaces without leaving HTML
resources.fullDescription: Tailwind CSS is a utility-first CSS framework that provides low-level utility classes to build custom designs without writing CSS. Instead of predefined components, Tailwind gives you building blocks (margin, padding, colors, flexbox, etc.) that you compose directly in your markup. Highly customizable via tailwind.config.js, with features like dark mode, responsive design, and automatic purging of unused styles. Used by GitHub, NASA, Shopify, and Laravel.
pros: No naming conventions needed (no BEM, SMACSS)
Consistent design system out of the box
cons: HTML can look cluttered with many classes
Learning curve for utility class names
Can be verbose for complex components

resources.name: Vite - Next Generation Build Tool
resources.shortDesc: Lightning-fast build tool with instant HMR and optimized production builds
resources.fullDescription: Vite (French for 'fast') is a modern build tool that provides blazing fast development experience through native ES modules and Hot Module Replacement (HMR). Created by Evan You (Vue.js creator), Vite serves source files over native ESM during development, resulting in instant server start regardless of app size. Production builds use Rollup for highly optimized bundles. Supports React, Vue, Svelte, and vanilla JavaScript.

specifications.Frameworks: React, Vue, Svelte, Vanilla JS, Preact, Lit
specifications.CSS: Supports CSS, PostCSS, Sass, Less, Stylus

features.JSX/TSX: React support out of the box

bestFor: Developers tired of slow webpack builds
